name: Using Examples
description: Learn how the live code examples work - preview, source, copy, Playground, multi-file examples, and recipes.

steps.title: Live Examples
steps.task: Most component and composable pages ship live examples - real Vue components running on the page, not screenshots.

steps.title: It's Live
steps.task: This is the real component running. We just selected "apple" to show it responding to state.

steps.title: Real Source
steps.task: Every example ships the exact code that produces it. We expanded it for you - the Expand pill collapses it again anytime.
steps.learn: Read the source behind an example

steps.title: Copy It
steps.task: This button copies the whole file to your clipboard in one click.

steps.title: Play With It
steps.task: This button opens the example live in the Playground - full editing, no setup.
steps.learn: Open an example in the Playground

steps.title: Tune the View
steps.task: The rest of the toolbar tunes the reading view - code size cycling and line wrap for long lines.
steps.learn: Adjust code size and line wrap

steps.title: Multiple Files
steps.task: Bigger examples span several real files - this one builds an async picker from a composable, a component, and the app wiring them together. We opened its code to show the file tabs.

steps.title: File Tabs
steps.task: Each tab is one source file of the same example - we switched to UserPicker.vue. The last file is the one running in the preview.

steps.title: Files Travel Together
steps.task: For multi-file examples, Play and Bin carry every file across, and the combine action stitches them into one scrollable view.
steps.learn: Open every file in Play or Bin at once

steps.title: Recipes
steps.task: Recipes are the other example type - terse, single-purpose snippets in a plain code fence, ready to lift straight into your app.

steps.title: Lift It
steps.task: Recipe fences are playground-ready too - one click and the snippet is running in Play.
steps.learn: Take a recipe into the Playground

steps.title: More Ways to Read
steps.task: Some pages show multiple variants in tabbed code, like this package-manager picker - we switched it to "npm" to show it in action.

steps.title: That's Examples
steps.task: Every page works this way - read it, copy it, play with it.
steps.learn: Use examples like a power user
